title: 10 best herbal teas for chronic hepatitis

teas.tea_desc: Milk thistle tea aids in treating chronic hepatitis because it contains silymarin, a flavonoid with anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. These properties protect liver cells from damage and promote liver regeneration. Scientific studies demonstrate that milk thistle extract significantly improves liver function in patients with hepatitis. The tea's active compounds reduce oxidative stress and inflammation, supporting the liver's overall health and well-being.
tea_parts: Seeds: The seeds of the milk thistle plant are used to make medicinal tea for chronic hepatitis due to their high concentration of silymarin, a flavonoid that is anti-inflammatory and protects the liver.
Leaves: Milk thistle leaves are used in medicinal tea for chronic hepatitis because they contain silymarin and other flavonoids that promote liver health and regeneration.
Flowers: Milk thistle flowers are used in medicinal tea for chronic hepatitis as they contain silymarin and other antioxidants that support liver function and detoxification.
Rhyzomes: Milk thistle rhyzomes are utilized in medicinal tea for chronic hepatitis because they have silymarin and other compounds that aid in liver protection and rejuvenation.
tea_recipe: Measure - Boil 8 ounces of water and let it cool down to 212 degrees Fahrenheit, then pour it over 1 tablespoon of dried milk thistle seeds.
Steep - Cover the container and let the milk thistle seeds steep for 15-20 minutes.
Strain - Strain the tea to remove the milk thistle seeds from the liquid.
Sweeten - Add honey or lemon to taste, if desired.
Consume - Drink the tea while it's still warm, up to three times a day for chronic hepatitis treatment.
tea_constituents: Silymarin: This flavonoid complex found in milk thistle has been shown to have strong antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects, which may help protect the liver from damage caused by chronic hepatitis.

Silybin: A major constituent of silymarin, silybin has been specifically studied for its hepatoprotective properties, showing promise in reducing liver enzyme levels and fibrosis in patients with chronic hepatitis.

Silydianin: Another component of silymarin, silydianin contributes to milk thistle's antioxidant activities, scavenging free radicals and helping to maintain liver health in individuals with chronic hepatitis.

Silychristin: The third main flavonolignan in silymarin, silychristin, works synergistically with silybin and silydianin to provide liver-protecting benefits, reducing oxidative stress and inflammation associated with chronic hepatitis.

teas.tea_desc: Dandelion tea aids chronic hepatitis due to its proven liver-protecting and regenerative properties. This herbal tea contains taraxacum officinale, which research shows stimulates bile production, promoting digestion and detoxification. Dandelion tea's antioxidants, such as vitamin C and luteolin, combat liver inflammation while reducing oxidative stress. Furthermore, studies reveal that this tea's potent antiviral agents inhibit hepatitis virus replication. Finally, dandelion tea's diuretic effect facilitates the removal of toxins from the body, easing the liver's workload and supporting overall hepatic health.
tea_parts: Roots: The roots of dandelions contain bitter compounds that stimulate the digestive system, promoting bile production and helping to improve liver function.
Leaves: The leaves of dandelions are a rich source of antioxidants, supporting the liver's ability to eliminate toxins and reducing inflammation.
Flowers: The flowers of dandelions are a source of lecithin, which supports liver function and helps to emulsify fats, reducing the burden on the liver.
tea_recipe: Pick 2-3 handfuls of fresh dandelion flowers from an organic and pesticide-free area, ensuring they are fully open and not wet from dew or rain.
Rinse the flowers gently under running water, then let them wilt in a cool place for a few hours to reduce their bitterness.
Coarsely chop the wilted dandelion flowers, then place them in a pot and add 4 cups of filtered water.
Slowly bring the water to a simmer over medium-low heat, then let it steep for 15-20 minutes with a lid on.
Strain the tea into a teapot or heat-resistant container, discarding the solids, and consume 3-4 cups daily, preferably between meals, for chronic hepatitis management.
tea_constituents: Taraxacin: A compound that has been shown to have a protective effect on liver cells, making it helpful in managing chronic hepatitis.
Taraxasterol: A plant sterol that has anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce liver inflammation in chronic hepatitis.
Inulin: A type of fiber that promotes the growth of beneficial gut bacteria, which can improve liver function and help manage chronic hepatitis.
Sesquiterpene lactones: A group of compounds that have been shown to have liver-protective effects and can help reduce liver damage in chronic hepatitis.
Flavonoids: A class of antioxidants that can help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the liver, making them helpful in managing chronic hepatitis.

teas.tea_desc: Turmeric tea contains curcumin, a polyphenol with established antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties. These properties help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the liver, key factors in chronic hepatitis. Curcumin also inhibits the replication of hepatitis viruses, slowing the progression of the disease. Additionally, it promotes the production of bile, supporting overall liver function. Lastly, turmeric tea's ability to improve blood flow aids the liver in removing toxins, further alleviating the symptoms of chronic hepatitis.
tea_parts: Rhizomes: The underground stem of turmeric contains curcumin, a potent antioxidant that supports liver health and helps fight inflammation in chronic hepatitis.
Leaves: Rich in essential oils and vitamins, turmeric leaves aid in detoxification and have hepatoprotective properties, beneficial for managing chronic hepatitis.
Roots: Turmeric roots are an abundant source of curcumin and other curcuminoids, which possess anti-fibrotic and anti-inflammatory effects, helping to alleviate chronic hepatitis symptoms.
Flowers: Turmeric flowers contain curcumin and have antioxidant properties, protecting liver cells from damage and promoting healing in chronic hepatitis.
Seeds: Turmeric seeds, though less common, still carry curcumin and have hepatoprotective benefits, assisting in treating chronic hepatitis by reducing oxidative stress.
tea_recipe: In a saucepan, combine 4 cups of water and 1 teaspoon of ground turmeric; bring the mixture to a boil, then reduce heat and simmer for 10 minutes.
Strain the tea through a fine-mesh sieve into a teapot or pitcher, pressing on the turmeric solids to extract all liquid.
Stir in 1-2 teaspoons of raw honey and 1/2 teaspoon of lemon juice into the turmeric tea for enhanced flavor and vitamin C.
Divide the tea into 4 cups and let it cool to a drinkable temperature.
Consume the turmeric tea twice daily for its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help support liver health in chronic hepatitis.
tea_constituents: Curcumin: The most active constituent in turmeric, curcumin has been shown to have anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and anti-fibrotic properties, which may help reduce liver inflammation and oxidative stress associated with chronic hepatitis.

Turmerone: A compound found in turmeric oil, turmerone has potent antioxidant and anti-inflammatory activities, which may help alleviate liver damage and promote liver cell regeneration in chronic hepatitis.

Demethoxycurcumin: A curcumin derivative, demethoxycurcumin exhibits anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and anti-fibrotic effects, which can help protect the liver from oxidative stress, inflammation, and fibrosis caused by chronic hepatitis.

Bisdemethoxycurcumin: Another curcumin derivative, bisdemethoxycurcumin has been found to have anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and anti-fibrotic properties, which can help improve liver function and reduce liver fibrosis in chronic hepatitis.

Curdione: A major component of turmeric volatile oil, curdione has demonstrated anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and hepatoprotective activities, which can help protect liver cells from damage and promote liver cell regeneration in chronic hepatitis.

teas.tea_desc: Artichoke tea, rich in caffeic acid and chlorogenic acid, neutralizes free radicals, supporting liver health. This tea also promotes bile production, aiding in the removal of toxins from the body. Its antioxidant properties protect liver cells from oxidative stress and damage. Artichoke tea, by stimulating bile excretion, assists in the elimination of harmful substances, alleviating symptoms of chronic hepatitis. Furthermore, this tea's anti-inflammatory agents reduce inflammation in the liver, providing relief for those suffering from chronic hepatitis.
tea_parts: Leaves: Rich in antioxidants, the artichoke leaves are known to protect and promote liver health, making them an ideal choice for medicinal tea to treat chronic hepatitis.
Buds: The artichoke buds possess potent anti-inflammatory and hepatoprotective properties, making them suitable for medicinal tea to support liver function in chronic hepatitis.
Rhyzomes: Artichoke rhyzomes contain bioactive compounds that aid in detoxification and liver regeneration, providing relief for those suffering from chronic hepatitis.
Seeds: Artichoke seeds are a powerful source of cynaropicrin, a compound known to stimulate bile production, improve digestion, and aid in treating chronic hepatitis.
tea_recipe: Choose 30-35 young artichokes, preferenceably organic, and rinse them thoroughly.
Trim the artichokes, removing leaves and excess stems, then cut in half and boil for 20 minutes.
Strain the artichokes, but reserve the liquid; blend the artichokes until a consistent puree forms.
Combine the artichoke puree with the reserved liquid, then simmer the mixture for an additional 20 minutes.
Strain the final mixture and let it cool; store the artichoke tea in the refrigerator for up to one week.
tea_constituents: Cynarin: This compound found in artichoke tea is known to stimulate bile production, which can help improve liver function and relieve symptoms of chronic hepatitis.

Silymarin: Also known as milk thistle extract, silymarin is a potent antioxidant that can help protect liver cells from damage and promote regeneration, making it beneficial for those with chronic hepatitis.
Chlorogenic acid: This polyphenol has anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce liver inflammation and oxidative stress, both of which are common in chronic hepatitis.

Caffeic acid: Another polyphenol found in artichoke tea, caffeic acid has been shown to have hepatoprotective effects, meaning it can help protect the liver from damage and support its function in those with chronic hepatitis.
Rutin: A flavonoid present in artichoke tea, rutin has antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce liver damage and improve liver function in those with chronic hepatitis.

teas.tea_desc: Burdock tea assists with chronic hepatitis due to its proven anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which reduce liver inflammation. This tea, derived from the burdock root, contains powerful compounds like arctiin and luteolin that protect liver cells from damage. By enhancing liver function, burdock tea promotes the elimination of toxins and waste products, further alleviating hepatitis symptoms. Additionally, burdock tea's ability to stimulate bile production aids in digestion and nutrient absorption, supporting overall liver health. Lastly, burdock tea's immune-boosting qualities combat oxidative stress, contributing to the healing process in individuals with chronic hepatitis.
tea_parts: Roots: The roots of the burdock plant are used in medicinal tea for chronic hepatitis due to their potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that can help support liver function.
Rhyzomes: These underground stems of the burdock plant are rich in beneficial compounds that have been shown to protect the liver and promote its health.
Leaves: The leaves of the burdock plant are known for their detoxifying properties, making them a valuable addition to a tea aimed at supporting liver health in cases of chronic hepatitis.
Seeds: The seeds of the burdock plant contain oils and nutrients that have been used in traditional medicine to treat liver ailments, including chronic hepatitis.
Buds: The unopened flowering tips, or buds, of the burdock plant possess potent therapeutic properties that can help stimulate liver function and promote overall health.
tea_recipe: Chop 2 tablespoons of dried burdock root into small pieces and rinse with water.
Place the burdock root in a pot and add 4 cups of water.
Bring the water to a boil, then reduce heat and simmer for 20 minutes.
Strain the tea to remove the burdock root pieces.
Drink the tea while warm, 2-3 times a day for best results.
tea_constituents: Arctiin: A lignan that has been shown to have hepatoprotective effects, potentially reducing inflammation and oxidative stress in the liver.
Arctigenin: A bioactive compound that may help reduce liver fibrosis and improve liver function in chronic hepatitis by suppressing inflammation and fibrosis-promoting factors.
Chlorogenic acid: A polyphenol that has antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, potentially aiding in liver protection and regeneration.
Quercetin: A flavonoid with antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects, which may contribute to reducing oxidative stress and liver injury in chronic hepatitis.
Rutin: A flavonoid known for its antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, potentially aiding in the prevention and treatment of liver damage caused by chronic hepatitis.
Resveratrol: A polyphenolic compound with antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects, which may help protect the liver from oxidative stress, fibrosis, and damage in chronic hepatitis.

teas.tea_desc: Yellowdock tea contains berberine, a potent alkaloid known for its antimicrobial and liver-protecting properties. This compound inhibits the replication of hepatitis viruses and promotes the regeneration of damaged liver cells. Yellowdock tea also boasts impressive anti-inflammatory and antioxidant abilities, reducing oxidative stress and inflammation in the liver. Furthermore, it supports the body's detoxification processes, facilitating the removal of harmful substances and promoting overall liver health. Regular consumption of yellowdock tea bolsters the immune system, enabling the body to combat hepatitis more effectively.
tea_parts: Roots: Rich in medicinal compounds, the roots of yellowdock possess potent liver-cleansing properties that aid in treating chronic hepatitis.
Rhizomes: Packed with medicinal benefits, yellowdock rhizomes contain ellagitannins and anthraquinones, making them an ideal addition to hepatitis-curing teas.
Leaves: Abundant in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory agents, yellowdock leaves support liver function and help combat chronic hepatitis.
Flowers: Adorned with healing qualities, yellowdock flowers serve as a gentle, yet effective ingredient in combating chronic hepatitis when prepared as a medicinal tea.
tea_recipe: Measure out 1-2 teaspoons of dried yellowdock root.
Place the root in a tea infuser or strainer.
Steep the yellowdock root in boiling water for 10-15 minutes.
Remove the infuser or strainer, discarding the used yellowdock root.
Consume the yellowdock tea, up to three times a day, for chronic hepatitis relief.
tea_constituents: Anthraquinones: These constituents have a laxative effect and can help eliminate toxins from the body, which can be beneficial for chronic hepatitis.
Tannins: Tannins have anti-inflammatory and astringent properties, which can help reduce inflammation and swelling in the liver, common symptoms of chronic hepatitis.
Chlorogenic acid: This compound has antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects, which can help protect liver cells from damage and reduce oxidative stress, common in chronic hepatitis.
Rutin: Rutin is a flavonoid with antioxidant properties that can help protect liver cells from damage and support liver function in chronic hepatitis.

teas.tea_desc: Peppermint tea provides relief for chronic hepatitis due to its anti-inflammatory and antiviral properties. The menthol and rosmarinic acid in peppermint tea inhibit the replication of viruses, including those causing hepatitis. These compounds also reduce inflammation, alleviating stress on the liver and promoting healing. Furthermore, peppermint tea stimulates bile flow and acts as a natural liver cleanser, aiding in the elimination of toxins. Its antioxidants protect liver cells from oxidative damage, contributing to overall liver health.
tea_parts: Leaves: Rich in essential oils and antioxidants, leaves of peppermint plant aid in soothing digestive issues and liver inflammation associated with chronic hepatitis.
Flowers: Packed with therapeutic compounds, peppermint flowers stimulate bile production, improving liver function in chronic hepatitis.
Stems: High in rosmarinic acid, peppermint stems have anti-inflammatory properties beneficial for treating liver diseases, such as chronic hepatitis.
Roots: Containing powerful bioactive compounds, peppermint roots promote liver health, reducing damage caused by chronic hepatitis.
tea_recipe: In a stove-top kettle, boil 8 cups of water to reach a rolling boil, which typically takes 10-15 minutes.
Crush or break 15 fresh organic peppermint leaves, or use 1 tablespoon of dried peppermint leaves, to release their oils.
Place the crushed or broken peppermint leaves in a teapot and immediately add the boiling water.
Cover the teapot and let the peppermint leaves steep for 5-10 minutes, depending on the desired strength.
Strain the tea into a cup, discarding the peppermint leaves, and enjoy this soothing beverage up to three times a day.
tea_constituents: Menthol: Has anti-inflammatory properties that may help reduce liver inflammation in chronic hepatitis.
Rosmarinic acid: Possesses antioxidant and anti-fibrotic effects, which may help protect the liver from oxidative stress and fibrosis in chronic hepatitis.
Flavonoids: Exhibit hepatoprotective effects by scavenging free radicals, inhibiting lipid peroxidation, and modulating liver enzymes in chronic hepatitis.
Tannins: Have astringent properties that can help protect liver tissues from damage and reduce inflammation in chronic hepatitis.
Essential oils: Possess antimicrobial and antiviral properties, which may help inhibit the replication of hepatitis viruses and reduce the risk of liver infections in chronic hepatitis.

teas.tea_desc: Ginger tea's anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, derived from its active compounds like gingerols and shogaols, provide significant liver protection. These compounds help reduce oxidative stress and hinder liver cell damage, contributing to improved liver function in chronic hepatitis. Furthermore, ginger tea demonstrates antiviral effects, inhibiting the replication of certain viruses known to cause hepatitis, providing a comprehensive approach to managing the disease. Additionally, ginger tea supports the immune system, enabling the body to better combat liver infections and inflammation. Lastly, ginger tea alleviates symptoms such as nausea and abdominal discomfort, enhancing overall well-being in individuals dealing with chronic hepatitis.
tea_parts: Roots: The roots of the ginger plant have anti-inflammatory properties that aid in reducing liver inflammation caused by chronic hepatitis.
Rhizomes: Ginger rhizomes are rich in antioxidants that help in protecting the liver from oxidative stress and damage due to chronic hepatitis.
Leaves: Ginger leaves contain polyphenols that exhibit hepatoprotective effects, helping to alleviate liver injury in chronic hepatitis.
Flowers: Ginger flowers have antiviral properties that can help inhibit the replication of the hepatitis virus, contributing to improved liver health.
Seeds: Ginger seeds possess antifibrotic properties, which assist in preventing liver scarring and cirrhosis caused by chronic hepatitis.
tea_recipe: Peel and grate 1 tablespoon of fresh ginger; steep in 1 cup of boiling water for 10 minutes.
Strain the ginger pieces from the water; add 1 teaspoon of honey and 1/2 lemon juice to the ginger water.
Warm the mixture on low heat until honey dissolves completely.
Let the tea cool down to a drinkable temperature.
Consume 2-3 times a day for chronic hepatitis.
tea_constituents: Gingerols: Gingerols, the main active compounds in ginger, have been found to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help reduce liver inflammation and protect liver cells from damage caused by chronic hepatitis.

Shogaols: Shogaols, another important component of ginger, have been shown to have hepatoprotective effects, meaning they can help protect the liver from damage and promote liver cell regeneration, which can be beneficial for individuals with chronic hepatitis.

Paradols: Paradols, a type of phenolic compound found in ginger, have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which may help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the liver, making them potentially helpful for managing chronic hepatitis.

Gingerdiols: Gingerdiols, a class of compounds in ginger, have been found to have antiviral properties, which may help inhibit the replication of viruses that contribute to chronic hepatitis, such as the hepatitis B virus (HBV) and hepatitis C virus (HCV).

Zingerone: Zingerone, a compound in ginger with antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, may help protect the liver from oxidative stress and inflammation, making it potentially helpful for managing chronic hepatitis.

teas.tea_desc: Chicory tea aids in alleviating chronic hepatitis due to its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which reduce liver inflammation. This tea, derived from the chicory plant's roots, contains inulin, a prebiotic fiber that promotes the growth of beneficial gut bacteria, enhancing overall gut health and immune function. Chicory tea also demonstrates hepatoprotective effects, shielding liver cells from oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals. Furthermore, it supports bile production and flow, improving digestion and elimination of toxins from the body, indirectly benefiting liver health. Finally, chicory tea's mild diuretic properties help maintain healthy fluid balance, reducing the workload on the liver.
tea_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, chicory roots help reduce liver inflammation and support tissue regeneration in chronic hepatitis.
Leaves: High in vitamin A and flavonoids, chicory leaves aid in maintaining healthy liver function and protecting liver cells from oxidative stress.
Flowers: Packed with phenolic acids and minerals, chicory flowers assist in promoting bile secretion and supporting overall liver health in chronic hepatitis.
Seeds: A natural source of cyanoringin and other beneficial compounds, chicory seeds protect the liver from damage and promote regeneration in chronic hepatitis.
tea_recipe: Chop 1 tablespoon of dried chicory root into small pieces, and place in a tea infuser or disposable tea bag.
Boil 2 cups of water, then pour over the chicory root in a teapot or heat-resistant container.
Steep the chicory root in hot water for 10-15 minutes, allowing the tea to develop a deep, earthy flavor.
Remove the tea infuser or tea bag, and discard the spent chicory root.
Serve the chicory tea warm or at room temperature, and enjoy up to 3 cups daily to support liver health.
tea_constituents: Inulin: Acts as a prebiotic, promoting the growth of beneficial bacteria in the gut, which can improve liver function and reduce inflammation associated with chronic hepatitis.
Sesquiterpene Lactones: Exhibit antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties that can protect liver cells from damage caused by free radicals and reduce inflammation in the liver.
Chicoric Acid: A polyphenol with antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects, can help reduce liver inflammation and protect liver cells from damage caused by oxidative stress.
Cichoric Acid: A type of phenolic acid with antioxidant properties, can help protect liver cells from damage caused by free radicals and reduce inflammation in the liver.
Tannins: Possess astringent properties, helping to reduce inflammation and protect liver cells from damage caused by oxidative stress.

teas.tea_desc: Licorice tea directly combats the hepatitis virus by inhibiting its replication, according to scientific studies. This tea also reduces inflammation in the liver, a key symptom of chronic hepatitis, due to its glycyrrhizin compound. Furthermore, licorice tea aids in detoxifying the body, which alleviates stress on the damaged liver. Lastly, this herbal tea boosts the immune system, helping the body fight off the hepatitis virus more effectively.
tea_parts: Roots: The roots of the licorice plant contain glycyrrhizin, which has anti-inflammatory and immune-stimulating properties that help protect the liver from chronic hepatitis.
Rhizomes: Licorice rhizomes have similar properties to the roots and are used interchangeably in medicinal teas, providing a source of triterpenoid saponins and flavonoids that aid in liver function.
Leaves: The leaves of the licorice plant contain isoliquiritigenin, a flavonoid with antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects, that helps in preventing liver damage due to chronic hepatitis.
tea_recipe: Boil 2 cups of water in a pot for 5 minutes.
Add 2 tablespoons of dried licorice root to the boiling water, then reduce heat and simmer for 10-15 minutes.
Strain the tea to remove the licorice root and discard it.
Let the tea cool down to a drinkable temperature.
Consume 1-2 cups of licorice tea per day, preferably between meals.
tea_constituents: Glycyrrhizin: This triterpenoid saponin found in licorice has anti-viral and anti-inflammatory properties, making it helpful in managing chronic hepatitis by reducing viral load and inflammation in the liver.
Liquiritigenin: This flavonoid in licorice has been shown to have hepatoprotective effects, meaning it helps protect the liver from damage, which can be beneficial for those with chronic hepatitis.
Glabridin: This isoflavan found in licorice has antioxidant properties that can help reduce oxidative stress in the liver, which is often elevated in individuals with chronic hepatitis.

Liquiritin: This flavonoid in licorice has been shown to have antifibrotic effects, which means it can help prevent or slow the progression of liver fibrosis, a common complication of chronic hepatitis.

other_remedies: Milk thistle, a flowering plant native to the Mediterranean, stands out as one of the most well-researched and effective natural remedies for chronic hepatitis. Its active compound, silymarin, has demonstrated potent antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties that protect the liver from further damage. Regular consumption of milk thistle extract, typically in capsule form, can improve liver function, reduce oxidative stress, and alleviate inflammation associated with chronic hepatitis. Another powerful natural remedy is Phyllanthus niruri, a plant commonly found in tropical regions. Also known as the "stone breaker," Phyllanthus niruri extract has displayed promising results in treating chronic hepatitis by inhibiting viral replication and promoting liver cell regeneration. Including this herb in one's daily routine, usually as a standardized extract, supports overall liver health and potentially decreases the severity of chronic hepatitis symptoms.
intro: Individuals suffering from chronic hepatitis often find solace in the soothing and therapeutic properties of herbal teas. Peppermint tea, rich in antioxidants, helps reduce inflammation and alleviate digestive discomfort associated with the condition. Dandelion root tea, known for its diuretic properties, supports the liver's natural detoxification processes and promotes healthy bile production. Meanwhile, turmeric tea, with its potent curcumin content, exhibits anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, helping to mitigate liver damage and promote overall healing.
definition: Chronic hepatitis is a long-term liver inflammation of at least six months, caused by hepatitis B, C, or D viruses. This condition steadily deteriorates liver function, potentially leading to severe complications such as cirrhosis, liver failure, and liver cancer. The impact on people's lives is substantial, as it often requires lifelong monitoring, treatments, and adjustments to daily activities. Chronic hepatitis affects not only physical health but also mental well-being, causing stress, anxiety, and decreased quality of life due to its unpredictable nature and potential long-term consequences.
